title: Desk with Drawer and Shelving
summary: A plywood-and-2x2 desk with a euro-slide drawer and two open shelves between its side panels, topped with a plank-and-trim top and a front leg apron.
description:
The two plywood sides are trimmed along their front edge, then joined by six 2x2 legs and front/back trim rails into a simple open frame — this is what carries the two shelves, one of which is optional if you'd rather leave that bay open. A single drawer sits in the upper-front opening, framed by 1x2 drawer trim and running on 16" euro-style white drawer slides.

The top is built from a wide plywood strip ("1x16") capped with 1x3 and 1x2 trim along the front edge, which is what gives the desktop a finished, slightly overhung look instead of a bare plywood edge. A single 1x4 leg apron ties the front legs together below the drawer for extra rigidity.

The drawer itself is a plain box — 1x3 sides and front/back, a plywood bottom, and a 1x4 drawer face attached last once the box is confirmed to run smoothly on its slides.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two sides at 28", two shelves at 24" and one top at 60" from the 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, 4x8 sheet. Cut four side trim at 15-3/4", two drawer trim at 24" and one top trim, narrow at 60" from the 1x2. Cut six legs at 30" and three front/back trim at 24" from the 2x2. Cut one top trim, wide at 60", two drawer front/back at 21-1/2" and two drawer sides at 16" from the 1x3. Cut one drawer bottom at 23" from the 1/4" plywood, 23 in x 16 in. Cut one drawer face at 23-3/4" and one leg apron at 15-3/4" from the 1x4. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 1x2, three 8 ft 2x2, two 8 ft 1x3 and one 4 ft 1x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the side panels
steps.body: Attach the side trim to both plywood side panels.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Join the six legs, front/back trim, and side panels into the open desk frame, then attach the leg apron below the drawer opening.

steps.title: Add the shelves and drawer trim
steps.body: Attach the two shelves and the drawer trim framing the drawer opening.

steps.title: Install the drawer slides
steps.body: Mount the euro-style drawer slides level in the drawer opening.

steps.title: Build the top
steps.body: Attach the top trim to the plywood top along its front edge.

steps.title: Build the drawer
steps.body: Assemble the drawer box from the front/back, sides, and plywood bottom, fit it to the slides, then attach the drawer face.

steps.title: Attach the top and add hardware
steps.body: Attach the top to the frame, then add the drawer knob or handle.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Desk with a drawer and two open shelves
